title: striga bilabiata subsp. barteri
intro: Striga bilabiata subsp. barteri is a species of flowering plant in the broomrape family, characterized by its twining stems and small, yellowish flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in West African folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Striga bilabiata subsp. barteri include relief from fever, headaches, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to treat wounds and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in decoctions or infusions, and its roots are sometimes dried and powdered for use in traditional remedies.
